sections.paragraph:
συναριθμέω pf. pass. ptc. συνηριθμημένος (Isaeus, Pla. et al.; POxy 1208, 17; Ex 12:4 συναριθμήσεται [fut. mid.] συνηριθμημένος) to include in a group as one among others, count/number together (Aristot., Eth. 1, 5; 2, 3; Plut., Mor. 1018f; Philo, Mos. 1, 278) ἔν τινι (Plut., Brut. 997 [29, 10]) ἐν τῷ εὐαγγελίῳ IPhld 5:2.—DELG s.v. ἀριθμός.

  1. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

Isaeus Isaeus , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

fut. fut. = fut.

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

IPhld IPhld = Ignatius to the Philadelphians—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 966
